title: Professional Summary
text:
ML Engineer with ~6 years total experience, 4 in production. Career progression across AI Engineering at a US startup (MirrorSize), founding engineer at a DRM startup (ttdsoft), graduate studies at IIIT Hyderabad, and production ML roles at Typeface (enterprise generative AI unicorn) and CARS24 (India's largest used-car marketplace). Delivers production systems across computer vision, generative AI, and cloud-native MLOps.

Shipped end-to-end pipelines from sub-5MB on-device TFLite models (running fully offline on Android devices with zero cloud latency) to Kubernetes inference clusters on GCP and Azure serving Fortune-500 clients. Comfortable owning the full stack: problem formulation, data annotation pipeline design (CVAT), dataset versioning, model training and evaluation, inference optimisation (ONNX, TFLite, Triton), API development (FastAPI), containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes, KServe), and production deployment with CI/CD (GitHub Actions).

Notable: Dean's List at IIIT Hyderabad, KVPY Scholar (IISc). Demonstrated preference for self-hosted, offline-first, privacy-respecting infrastructure.

title: M.Tech — IIIT Hyderabad
text:
Master of Technology (M.Tech) in Computer Science from IIIT Hyderabad (August 2021 to May 2023). IIIT Hyderabad is consistently ranked among India's top 3 research institutes for computer science and AI.

Coursework: Natural Language Processing (transformer architectures, sequence modelling, attention mechanisms), Statistical AI (probabilistic graphical models, Bayesian inference, reinforcement learning), Distributed Systems (consensus algorithms, fault tolerance, distributed databases), Blockchain and Cryptocurrencies (consensus protocols, smart contracts, decentralised applications), Neural and Cognitive Modelling (biologically-inspired neural networks, cognitive architectures).

Graduated on the Dean's List — awarded to the top percentile of students for academic excellence.

title: B.Tech — IIIT Delhi
text:
Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in Computer Science from IIIT Delhi (August 2015 to May 2019). IIIT Delhi is a government-funded institute of national importance under the Act of Parliament, known for its strong industry-oriented computer science curriculum and entrepreneurship culture.

Coursework: Computer Vision (image processing, feature extraction, object detection and recognition), Machine Learning (supervised and unsupervised learning, model evaluation, bias-variance tradeoff), GPU Computing (CUDA programming, parallel algorithms, GPU architecture optimisation), Image Analysis (segmentation, morphological operations, texture analysis).

title: Skills: Languages & AI/ML
text:
Languages: Python (primary, 6+ years), C, C++ (DRM systems, CUDA kernels), JavaScript, TypeScript (full-stack and Electron apps).

AI/ML Frameworks: PyTorch (primary deep learning framework), OpenCV (image processing pipelines), Scikit-Learn (classical ML), ONNX (model interchange and optimisation), TFLite / TensorFlow Lite (on-device mobile inference with int8 quantisation), Triton Inference Server (production model serving with auto-scaling).

LLM / RAG Stack: LangChain (RAG orchestration and agentic workflows), Qdrant (production vector search), RAGAS (RAG evaluation and benchmarking), LLM-as-judge evaluation for faithfulness, relevance, and hallucination metrics, Sentence Transformers (embedding models), Groq (ultra-fast LLM inference), OpenAI API, Anthropic / Claude.

title: Skills: Web, Cloud & Infrastructure
text:
Web & Backend: FastAPI (async handlers, SSE streaming, REST), Django (ORM-heavy applications), Flask (lightweight APIs), Node.js (DRM licensing server, encryption APIs), React.js (component-based UIs), Next.js (full-stack React with SSR), Electron.js (cross-platform desktop applications with native OS integration).

Cloud & MLOps: Docker (containerisation, multi-stage builds, slim images), Docker Compose (multi-service orchestration), Kubernetes (production cluster management, auto-scaling), KServe (Kubernetes-native model serving with custom predictors), GCP (GKE, Vertex AI, Cloud Build), Azure (AKS, VM-based inference), Proxmox (self-hosted hypervisor and homelab), GitHub Actions (CI/CD pipelines with quality gates).

Databases & Storage: PostgreSQL (primary relational DB), ChromaDB (embedding store for RAG), Redis (caching, semantic cache with TTL), Qdrant (production vector search), FAISS (approximate nearest neighbour indexing with HNSW, IVF).

Specialties: On-device inference optimisation (TFLite, model pruning, int8 quantisation, sub-5MB binaries), model serving (Triton, KServe, custom FastAPI containers with auto-scaling), RAG pipeline design (chunking strategies, embedding selection, hybrid search, relevance thresholding), MLOps automation (CI/CD for ML, dataset versioning, automated retraining), self-hosted and privacy-preserving AI infrastructure (Ollama, offline-first architectures).

title: Complete Tech Stack
text:
Kshitij Gupta's complete technology stack and technical expertise:

Languages: Python, C, C++, JavaScript, TypeScript.

AI/ML Frameworks: PyTorch, OpenCV, Scikit-Learn, ONNX, TFLite, Triton Inference Server, Hugging Face Transformers, Sentence Transformers.

LLM / RAG: LangChain, RAGAS, LLM-as-judge, Qdrant, ChromaDB, FAISS, Ollama, Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic.

Cloud & Infrastructure: Docker, Docker Compose, Kubernetes, KServe, GCP (GKE, Vertex AI, Cloud Build), Azure (AKS), Proxmox, GitHub Actions, CI/CD, systemd.

Databases: PostgreSQL, Redis, Qdrant, ChromaDB, FAISS.

MLOps: Model serving (Triton, KServe), model optimisation (int8 quantisation, pruning, ONNX conversion), on-device inference (TFLite), data pipelines (CVAT, DVC), automated deployment, monitoring.

Specialties: Computer vision, generative AI, RAG systems, on-device ML, self-hosted AI infrastructure, privacy-preserving ML, MLOps automation, cross-platform desktop (Electron), DRM systems.

title: CARS24 — ML Engineer
text:
ML Engineer at CARS24 (India's largest used-car marketplace, processing thousands of vehicle inspections daily across 200+ cities), Gurgaon. June 2024 – June 2025.

1. Image Validation Pipeline:
Designed and deployed end-to-end image validation for per-part car inspection via KServe + Triton Inference Server on GCP Kubernetes (GKE). Validates hundreds of images per vehicle in real-time — checking for quality, completeness, and damage markers. Cut per-vehicle inspection time by 20-30 minutes, eliminating manual image review bottlenecks. Handles thousands of concurrent inspections with auto-scaling inference pods.

2. On-device TFLite model:
Engineered on-device TFLite model for part classification and pose detection — full binary under 5MB (int8 quantised, pruned, ONNX-to-TFLite converted). Runs fully offline on budget Android devices used by field inspectors across 200+ Indian cities. Zero cloud round-trip latency, sub-100ms inference per image.

3. Underbody oil-leak detection:
Extended damage-estimation pipeline with computer vision module for detecting oil leaks from underbody images. Improved damage prediction accuracy and added coverage for a previously undetected damage category.

4. Full MLOps lifecycle:
Owned complete MLOps lifecycle: CVAT annotation schema design → DVC dataset versioning → Vertex AI training → GitHub Actions automated cloud deployment. Reduced model iteration cycles from weeks to days by eliminating manual handoffs across teams.

title: Typeface India — Software Engineer
text:
Software Engineer at Typeface (enterprise generative AI unicorn, $1B+ valuation), Hyderabad. July 2023 – March 2024.

1. Fortune-500 generative image features:
Shipped production generative-image features for Fortune-500 clients (LG, Marks & Spencer, Ford). Owned full pipeline from model integration to deployed inference — prompt engineering, output quality evaluation, latency optimisation, and production rollout. Used in campaigns reaching millions of end-users.

2. Multi-model integration:
Integrated and productionised Stable Diffusion (self-hosted on Azure AKS), Google ImageGen (GCP Vertex AI), and DALL-E (OpenAI). Built unified abstraction layer enabling cost/latency/quality-based model switching without changing downstream pipeline code. Pipelines included scene generation (product-in-context synthesis), inpainting (object removal), outpainting (canvas expansion), colour-palette extraction, and style-transfer.

3. High-availability inference:
Maintained inference infrastructure across Azure AKS (self-hosted models) and GCP Vertex AI (managed services). Implemented fallback routing, request queuing, and auto-scaling. Met 99.5% uptime SLA with sub-2s median latency for Fortune-500 clients.

title: ttdsoft — Software Engineer (Founding)
text:
Founding Software Engineer at ttdsoft (early-stage DRM startup for content creators and distributors), New Delhi. May 2020 – June 2021.

1. Cross-platform DRM suite:
Built DRM ecosystem spanning Windows (C++/Qt, kernel-level encryption hooks), macOS (Swift, FileVault integration), and Android (Kotlin, Android Keystore). Products included ePendrive (USB drive with hardware-locked encrypted content distribution), Secure Browser (locked-down Chromium preventing copying, printing, and screen-capture), and encrypted video player with DRM-protected streaming. Re-architected core product stack from Python (PyQt) to Electron.js (TypeScript + React), improving UI performance, reducing memory footprint, and enabling seamless cross-platform builds.

2. Licensing and encryption API:
Built Node.js REST API for license generation, hardware binding, and content encryption key management. Handled activation/deactivation, offline grace periods, and device limits. Self-hosted entire backend on bare-metal — PostgreSQL, API servers, email OTP, license validation — with zero cloud spend and zero vendor dependency.

title: MirrorSize US Inc. — AI Engineer
text:
AI Engineer at MirrorSize (US-based virtual fitting room technology startup for e-commerce), New Delhi. May 2019 – June 2020.

Built end-to-end person-segmentation and body-measurement estimation pipeline using Mask R-CNN. Takes a single RGB image from a standard smartphone camera and performs: person segmentation (isolating person from background), body-contour extraction (identifying shoulders, waist, hips, inseam landmarks), and body-measurement estimation (predicting bust, waist, hip, and inseam dimensions from single 2D image). Enabled virtual fit recommendations without specialist 3D scanning hardware. Built with PyTorch and OpenCV, with custom post-processing for measurement regression from segmentation masks.

Kshitij's first professional AI/ML position — gained deep experience in full computer vision pipeline: dataset collection and curation, model training and evaluation, deployment to production inference API.

title: LLM Eval Harness
text:
Automated evaluation pipeline for RAG and agentic LLM systems. Python, FastAPI, ChromaDB, PostgreSQL.

YAML-driven evaluation harness supporting 4 LLM providers (Ollama, Groq, OpenAI, Claude) with LLM-as-judge scoring for faithfulness, relevance, hallucination rate, and latency metrics.

Implemented a full RAG evaluation pipeline using ChromaDB and sentence-transformers for document ingestion, embedding, retrieval, and automated benchmarking across multiple models.

Developed a FastAPI dashboard, HTML reporting system, and GitHub Actions CI/CD gate that blocks deployments when hallucination thresholds regress beyond configured limits.

title: Image Similarity Search
text:
CLIP + FAISS based semantic image retrieval engine. Python, FastAPI, PyTorch, FAISS.

Built a production-style image similarity search system using CLIP ViT-B-32 embeddings and FAISS ANN indexing to retrieve semantically similar images with sub-millisecond search latency.

Implemented HNSW, IVF, and exact FAISS indexes with benchmarking for recall vs latency trade-offs; added incremental indexing, metadata filtering, and embedding caching.

Developed a FastAPI backend and Streamlit frontend supporting image uploads, top-K retrieval, similarity scoring, metrics, and real-time semantic search visualisation.

title: NeuralDocs AI
text:
Local-first AI document intelligence platform. FastAPI, Ollama, ChromaDB, Docker, Next.js.

Self-hosted PDF analyser running LLM inference locally via Ollama — zero data egress; deployable on VPS, Proxmox, or bare-metal via docker compose up. Delivers summaries, structured Q&A, and document insights.

title: ONNXLab
text:
Browser-native ONNX visualisation and inference platform. Next.js, TypeScript, React Flow, ONNX Runtime Web.

Built an interactive ONNX model visualisation platform with dynamic graph rendering, tensor metadata inspection, and Netron-inspired node analysis using React Flow and Dagre.

Implemented fully client-side ONNX inference in the browser using ONNX Runtime Web with automatic WebGPU/WASM fallback for GPU-accelerated execution.

Developed a dynamic tensor input pipeline supporting image preprocessing, tensor analysis, top-K prediction extraction, and custom label mapping for classification models.

title: Honours & Awards
text: Dean's List, IIIT Hyderabad (M.Tech) | KVPY Scholar 2014 (IISc) | Google Lunar X-Prize Moonbots Top-30 Finalist | JSTSE Scholar 2012 (Delhi Government).

title: Job Availability & Preferences
text:
Currently open to new opportunities and actively looking for roles applying ML engineering and MLOps experience.

Preferred roles: ML Engineer (production ML / inference serving), MLOps Engineer (infrastructure, CI/CD for ML, model deployment), Backend Engineer (Python, API design with AI/ML components), AI Infrastructure Engineer (self-hosted and cloud inference infrastructure). Open to adjacent roles like Applied Scientist (production engineering focus) or Developer Advocate (ML/MLOps tooling).

Engagement: Full-time or contract / freelance — open to both.
Notice period: Immediate to 2 weeks (currently not employed).
Location: Open to remote worldwide. Willing to relocate for right role. Based in New Delhi (IST, UTC+5:30).
Work authorisation: Authorised to work in India. Open to international remote as independent contractor.
Languages: English (fluent), Hindi (native).

title: Interests & Personality
text:
Hobbies & interests:
- Gaming: Strategy and simulation games. Appreciates game design, systems thinking, and optimisation challenges.
- Movies, TV, and anime: Sci-fi, thriller, psychological drama, slice-of-life. Enjoys analysing storytelling and world-building.
- Open-source: Contributes to and maintains projects. Believes in building tools other developers find useful.

What excites you technically:
- On-device and edge AI inference: Making models small, fast, and reliable enough to run without a cloud connection.
- Systems programming and tooling: Building developer tools that make other engineers more productive — model visualisers, eval harnesses, CI/CD pipelines.
- MLOps infrastructure: Automated deployment, monitoring, retraining, and quality gates.
- Self-hosted and offline-first: Privacy, control, and running software on own infrastructure without vendor lock-in.

Working style: Async-first (prefers written communication for decisions and design docs), documentation-driven (architecture decisions, runbooks, API specs), ownership mindset (design → implementation → testing → deployment → post-mortem). Prefers small, focused teams over large, process-heavy organisations.

Fun fact: At CARS24, optimised a TFLite model down to 4.8MB — small enough to fit on a 5MB email attachment — deployed to thousands of Android devices across 200+ Indian cities. Runs entirely offline, processing images in sub-100ms on budget Android phones.

title: Engineering Philosophy
text: Believes engineering should prioritise reliability, simplicity, and ownership. Prefers building systems that are observable, reproducible, and easy to operate in production. Strong preference for privacy-respecting, self-hosted, and offline-first architectures when practical. Values automation over manual processes, especially in ML workflows. Enjoys working across the entire stack — from model development to deployment infrastructure — and is motivated by solving real-world constraints involving latency, cost, scale, and maintainability.

title: Why Hire Kshitij
text: Combines experience across computer vision, generative AI, backend engineering, and MLOps rather than specialising in only one layer of the stack. Has delivered production systems ranging from sub-5MB mobile models to Kubernetes inference clusters serving enterprise workloads. Comfortable owning projects end-to-end including data pipelines, model development, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and infrastructure. Strong track record across startups, enterprise AI products, and self-hosted systems with a focus on practical business impact.
